assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies this as cert-manager's webhook certificates not being compatible with the new API server version and causing admission webhook errors

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Includes command 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' to identify the webhook

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides immediate mitigation by patching the webhook to set failurePolicy to Ignore, temporarily disabling strict validation

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Does not provide a kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration; only shows patching to change failurePolicy

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Warns that the mitigation is temporary and emphasizes restoring normal operation with strict validation later

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions checking current cert-manager version and finding compatible versions, noting cert-manager 1.13+ supports K8s 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Provides complete helm upgrade commands to upgrade cert-manager to v1.15.3 which supports K8s 1.32 (expectation says 1.30 but 1.32 is the actual version mentioned in prompt)

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: The immediate mitigation uses kubectl patch to set failurePolicy to Ignore as the primary solution

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Includes multiple pod creation tests: 'kubectl run test-pod' after immediate fix and 'kubectl run test-pod-2' after permanent fix

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Does not mention the need to persist changes in source of truth systems like GitOps manifests or CI/CD pipelines

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Clearly labels the failurePolicy change as temporary mitigation and includes steps to restore strict validation by reverting to 'Fail' after the permanent fix
